history.qwen_observation: The UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 recipe removed version management for problem-spring-web and jjwt dependencies, causing Maven to fail POM parsing due to missing <version> tags.
history.qwen_rationale: Insert a pom_patch step immediately after the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade to explicitly set versions for the unmanaged dependencies (problem-spring-web and jjwt artifacts) so the POM parses and compiles successfully under JDK 17 before proceeding to Java 17/21 transforms.

history.qwen_observation: Build failed post-recipe at `upgrade_spring_boot_3_3` due to missing `<version>` tags for `problem-spring-web` and `jjwt` artifacts, which lost their managed versions after the Spring Boot parent upgrade.
history.qwen_rationale: Moving the `pom_patch_fix_missing_versions` step before `upgrade_spring_boot_3_3` ensures the missing dependency versions are explicitly declared in the POM before the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade recipe runs and triggers a compile check, preventing the immediate Maven resolution failure.

history.qwen_observation: The Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade step fails to compile because Spring Security 6 removed `WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter`, and dependencies like `problem-spring-web`, `jjwt`, and `elasticsearch` are missing or version-misaligned after the framework bump.
history.qwen_rationale: Merging the WSCA AST rewrite and dependency version fixes directly into the `upgrade_spring_boot_3_3` step ensures they run before the post-step `mvn compile` check, preventing the chain from aborting. Switching from `pom_patch` to `UpgradeDependencyVersion` recipes avoids lxml DOM manipulation quirks and lets OpenRewrite safely resolve versions within the upgraded dependency tree.

history.qwen_observation: The Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade step failed because Maven reported missing <version> tags for problem-spring-web and jjwt artifacts, which are not managed by the Spring Boot BOM and were left unversioned after the recipe run.
history.qwen_rationale: Inserting a pom_patch step before the Spring Boot upgrade forces explicit versions on these unmanaged dependencies at the XML level, guaranteeing a valid POM before mvn compile executes. The redundant UpgradeDependencyVersion recipes are removed from the upgrade step to prevent recipe-matching conflicts and streamline the transition.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed after Spring Boot 3 upgrade due to missing `ProblemModule` (Zalando problem-spring-web) and `org.elasticsearch.index.query` (Elasticsearch client) on the classpath.
history.qwen_rationale: The `UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3` recipe likely removed or failed to manage these third-party dependencies during the javax-to-jakarta transition. Explicitly adding `problem-spring-web` (0.29.1, compatible with SB3/Jakarta) and `elasticsearch` (7.17.24, to preserve legacy query APIs) via `AddDependency` in the same step ensures they are resolved before the post-recipe compile check runs.
